sections.label: Section 5.1
sections.original: Cur non , Mopse , boni quoniam convenimus ambo ,
sections.translation: Why , Mopsus , being both together met ,

sections.label: Section 5.2
sections.original: tu calamos inflare levis , ego dicere versus ,
sections.translation: you skilled to breathe upon the slender reeds ,

sections.label: Section 5.3
sections.original: hic corylis mixtas inter consedimus ulmos ?
sections.translation: I to sing ditties , do we not sit down

sections.label: Section 5.4
sections.original: Tu maior ; tibi me est aequum parere , Menalca ,
sections.translation: here where the elm-trees and the hazels blend ?

sections.label: Section 5.5
sections.original: sive sub incertas zephyris motantibus umbras ,
sections.translation: You are the elder , ' tis for me to bide

sections.label: Section 5.6
sections.original: sive antro potius succedimus : aspice , ut antrum
sections.translation: your choice , Menalcas , whether now we seek

sections.label: Section 5.7
sections.original: silvestris raris sparsit labrusca racemis .
sections.translation: yon shade that quivers to the changeful breeze ,

sections.label: Section 5.8
sections.original: Montibus in nostris solus tibi certat Amyntas .
sections.translation: or the cave ' s shelter . Look you how the cave

sections.label: Section 5.9
sections.original: Quid , si idem certet Phoebum superare canendo ?
sections.translation: is with the wild vine ' s clusters over-laced !

sections.label: Section 5.10
sections.original: Incipe , Mopse , prior , si quos aut Phyllidis ignes ,
sections.translation: None but Amyntas on these hills of ours

sections.label: Section 5.11
sections.original: aut Alconis habes laudes , aut iurgia Codri :
sections.translation: can vie with you .

sections.label: Section 5.12
sections.original: incipe , pascentis servabit Tityrus haedos .
sections.translation: What if he also strive

sections.label: Section 5.13
sections.original: Immo haec , in viridi nuper quae cortice fagi
sections.translation: to out-sing Phoebus ?

sections.label: Section 5.14
sections.original: carmina descripsi et modulans alterna notavi ,
sections.translation: Do you first begin ,

sections.label: Section 5.15
sections.original: experiar , tu deinde iubeto ut certet Amyntas .
sections.translation: good Mopsus , whether minded to sing aught

sections.label: Section 5.16
sections.original: Lenta salix quantum pallenti cedit olivae ,
sections.translation: of Phyllis and her loves , or Alcon ' s praise ,

sections.label: Section 5.17
sections.original: puniceis humilis quantum saliunca rosetis ,
sections.translation: or to fling taunts at Codrus . Come , begin ,

sections.label: Section 5.18
sections.original: iudicio nostro tantum tibi cedit Amyntas .
sections.translation: while Tityrus watches o ' er the grazing kids .

sections.label: Section 5.19
sections.original: sed tu desine plura , puer ; successimus antro .
sections.translation: Nay , then , I will essay what late I carved

sections.label: Section 5.20
sections.original: Extinctum nymphae crudeli funere Daphnim
sections.translation: on a green beech-tree ' s rind , playing by turns ,

sections.label: Section 5.21
sections.original: flebant ; vos coryli testes et flumina nymphis ;
sections.translation: and marking down the notes ; then afterward

sections.label: Section 5.22
sections.original: cum complexa sui corpus miserabile nati ,
sections.translation: bid you Amyntas match them if he can .

sections.label: Section 5.23
sections.original: atque deos atque astra vocat crudelia mater .
sections.translation: As limber willow to pale olive yields ,

sections.label: Section 5.24
sections.original: Non ulli pastos illis egere diebus
sections.translation: as lowly Celtic nard to rose-buds bright ,

sections.label: Section 5.25
sections.original: frigida , Daphni , boves ad flumina ; nulla neque amnem
sections.translation: so , to my mind , Amyntas yields to you .

sections.label: Section 5.26
sections.original: libavit quadrupes , nec graminis attigit herbam .
sections.translation: But hold awhile , for to the cave we come .

sections.label: Section 5.27
sections.original: Daphni , tuum Poenos etiam ingemuisse leones
sections.translation: “ For Daphnis cruelly slain wept all the Nymphs —

sections.label: Section 5.28
sections.original: interitum montesque feri silvaeque loquuntur .
sections.translation: ye hazels , bear them witness , and ye streams —

sections.label: Section 5.29
sections.original: Daphnis et Armenias curru subiungere tigres
sections.translation: when she , his mother , clasping in her arms

sections.label: Section 5.30
sections.original: instituit ; Daphnis thiasos inducere Bacchi ,
sections.translation: the hapless body of the son she bare ,

sections.label: Section 6.1
sections.original: Prima Syracosio dignata est ludere versu ,
sections.translation: first my Thalia stooped in sportive mood

sections.label: Section 6.2
sections.original: nostra nec erubuit silvas habitare Thalia .
sections.translation: to Syracusan strains , nor blushed within

sections.original: Cum canerem reges et proelia , Cynthius aurem
sections.translation: the woods to house her . When I sought to tell

sections.label: Section 6.4
sections.original: vellit , et admonuit : “ Pastorem , Tityre , pinguis
sections.translation: of battles and of kings , the Cynthian god

sections.label: Section 6.5
sections.original: pascere oportet ovis , deductum dicere carmen .”
sections.translation: plucked at mine ear and warned me : “ Tityrus ,

sections.label: Section 6.6
sections.original: Nunc ego — namque super tibi erunt , qui dicere laudes ,
sections.translation: beseems a shepherd-wight to feed fat sheep ,

sections.label: Section 6.7
sections.original: Vare , tuas cupiant , et tristia condere bella —
sections.translation: but sing a slender song .” Now , Varus , I —

sections.label: Section 6.8
sections.original: agrestem tenui meditabor arundine Musam .
sections.translation: for lack there will not who would laud thy deeds ,

sections.original: Non iniussa cano : si quis tamen haec quoque , si quis
sections.translation: and treat of dolorous wars — will rather tune

sections.label: Section 6.10
sections.original: captus amore leget , te nostrae , Vare , myricae ,
sections.translation: to the slim oaten reed my silvan lay .

sections.label: Section 6.11
sections.original: te nemus omne canet ; nec Phoebo gratior ulla est ,
sections.translation: I sing but as vouchsafed me ; yet even this

sections.label: Section 6.12
sections.original: quam sibi quae Vari praescripsit pagina nomen .
sections.translation: if , if but one with ravished eyes should read ,

sections.label: Section 6.13
sections.original: Pergite , Pierides ! Chromis et Mnasyllos in antro
sections.translation: of thee , O Varus , shall our tamarisks

sections.original: Silenum pueri somno videre iacentem ,
sections.translation: and all the woodland ring ; nor can there be

sections.original: inflatum hesterno venas , ut semper , Iaccho :
sections.translation: a page more dear to Phoebus , than the page

sections.label: Section 6.16
sections.original: serta procul tantum capiti delapsa iacebant ,
sections.translation: where , foremost writ , the name of Varus stands .

sections.label: Section 6.17
sections.original: et gravis attrita pendebat cantharus ansa .
sections.translation: Speed ye , Pierian Maids ! Within a cave

sections.original: Adgressi — nam saepe senex spe carminis ambo
sections.translation: young Chromis and Mnasyllos chanced to see

sections.label: Section 6.19
sections.original: luserat — iniciunt ipsis ex vincula sertis :
sections.translation: silenus sleeping , flushed , as was his wont ,

sections.label: Section 6.20
sections.original: addit se sociam , timidisque supervenit Aegle ,—
sections.translation: with wine of yesterday . Not far aloof ,

sections.label: Section 6.21
sections.original: Aegle , Naiadum pulcherrima ,— iamque videnti
sections.translation: slipped from his head , the garlands lay , and there

sections.label: Section 6.22
sections.original: sanguineis frontem moris et tempora pingit .
sections.translation: by its worn handle hung a ponderous cup .

sections.original: Ille dolum ridens , “ Quo vincula nectitis ?” inquit ;
sections.translation: Approaching — for the old man many a time

sections.label: Section 6.24
sections.original: “ solvite me , pueri ; satis est potuisse videri :
sections.translation: had balked them both of a long hoped-for song —

sections.label: Section 6.25
sections.original: carmina , quae voltis , cognoscite ; carmina vobis ,
sections.translation: garlands to fetters turned , they bind him fast .

sections.label: Section 6.26
sections.original: huic aliud mercedis erit .” Simul incipit ipse .
sections.translation: Then Aegle , fairest of the Naiad-band ,

sections.original: Tum vero in numerum Faunosque ferasque videres
sections.translation: aegle came up to the half-frightened boys ,

sections.original: ludere , tum rigidas motare cacumina quercus ;
